_comment: Infrastructure registry for AI automation agents. Copy this to infra.json and fill in all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values. See docs/setup-new-project.md for step-by-step instructions.
_region_comment: AWS region where your Lambda functions and DynamoDB tables live (e.g. us-east-1)
_adoOrg_comment: Your Azure DevOps organization URL (e.g. https://yourorg.visualstudio.com). No trailing slash.
_adoProject_comment: Primary ADO project name where pipelines are registered (e.g. My Engineering Platform)
_pipelineFolder_comment: Optional ADO pipeline folder path (e.g. \\MyFolder). Pipelines are created inside this folder for organization. Leave empty or omit for root folder.

pipelines._comment: Pipeline IDs are assigned by ADO when you import the YAML files. Record them here after import.

dor.name: {{PIPELINE_NAME_PREFIX}}-DOR-Agent
dor._name_comment: Hub: KAI (from pipelineFolder). Consumer: KAI-<RepoShortName> (e.g. KAI-MyApp). Names must be unique across repos in the same ADO project.
dor._id_comment: ADO pipeline definition ID. Find it in ADO > Pipelines > pipeline > Edit > URL (?definitionId=XXXX)

pr-answer.name: {{PIPELINE_NAME_PREFIX}}-PR-Answer-Agent
pr-answer.note: Separate from pr-review — this one responds to comments on YOUR PRs

lambdas._comment: Two Lambda functions handle all webhook traffic. WI Router handles all workitem.updated events (1 route: POST /wi), PR Router handles PR comment events (1 route: POST /pr-answer). Deployed via lambda/deploy.sh.

wi-router._functionName_comment: Single Lambda for all work item webhooks. Single API Gateway route POST /wi points here. Tag-based routing determines which agent to invoke.

pr-router._functionName_comment: Handles PR comment webhooks. API Gateway route /pr-answer points here.

storage._comment: AWS resource names. Convention: use your PIPELINE_NAME_PREFIX in lowercase as a resource name prefix (e.g. myai-dedupe).

dedupe._tableName_comment: DynamoDB table for webhook event deduplication. TTL-based (1 hour).

rateLimits._tableName_comment: DynamoDB table for daily per-pipeline rate limit counters.

tokenBudget._tableName_comment: DynamoDB table for monthly token usage tracking.

dlq._queueName_comment: SQS dead letter queue for failed Lambda events. 14-day retention.

bundles._bucketName_comment: S3 bucket for run bundle artifacts. Bucket name must be globally unique — appending account ID ensures this.

iamPolicy._policyName_comment: IAM policy name granting Lambda access to DynamoDB, SQS, S3.
iamPolicy._attachedTo_comment: IAM role that Lambda functions run under. Find in AWS Console > Lambda > function > Configuration > Permissions.

snsTopic.name: {{RESOURCE_PREFIX}}-alerts
snsTopic._name_comment: SNS topic for CloudWatch alarm notifications.

monitoring._alarms_comment: CloudWatch alarm names — must match what is defined in lambda/cloudwatch/alarms.json

apiGateway._comment: HTTP API Gateway that routes webhook traffic to Lambda. Created once by your infra team. URL never changes after creation.
apiGateway.name: {{PIPELINE_NAME_PREFIX}}-Agent
apiGateway._id_comment: 10-char API Gateway ID. Find in AWS Console > API Gateway > your API > ID column.

webhooks._comment: ADO Service Hooks. WI hooks (User Story + Bug) are project-scoped (hub only). PR Answer hook is per-repo (hub + each consumer) — filtered to repository + base branch to avoid firing on all repos.

pr-answer._scope_comment: Per-repo hook — each repo (hub + consumers) creates its own, filtered to repository + base branch. Avoids firing on all PR comments across the ADO project.

_dependencies.code-change: Run deploy.sh → Lambda updated (URL stays same)
_dependencies.lambda-name-change: Update lambdas.*.functionName here → redeploy → re-link in API Gateway
_dependencies.gateway-change: Update apiGateway here → update ADO Service Hook URL
_dependencies.webhook-change: Update in ADO Service Hooks console (no local file to change)
